redFlags.title: 12A/12AB Registration Expired or Not Renewed
redFlags.description: Charitable trust exemption u/s 11/12 requires valid 12A (old) or 12AB (new) registration. Since FY 2022-23, all trusts must hold 12AB registration. Expired or cancelled registration means no exemption.
redFlags.consequence: Full income taxed at maximum marginal rate (30%) with no charitable deduction. Back-taxes for years registration was invalid.
redFlags.correction: Renew 12AB registration on Income Tax portal well before expiry. Current registrations valid for 5 years.

redFlags.title: Less Than 85% of Income Applied for Charitable Purposes
redFlags.description: Section 11(1)(a) requires minimum 85% application of income for charitable/religious purposes. Unapplied income exceeding 15% is taxed at 30% unless accumulated per Section 11(2) via Form 10/9A.
redFlags.consequence: Tax at 30% on shortfall amount plus interest and potential penalties.
redFlags.correction: Increase application before year-end OR file Form 10 (accumulation for specific purpose, max 5 years) before return due date.

redFlags.title: Form 10B Not Filed When Income Exceeds ₹1 Crore
redFlags.description: Trusts with total income > ₹1 Crore must have accounts audited by a CA and submit Form 10B (audit report). Form 10BB for institutions u/s 10(23C).
redFlags.consequence: Penalty u/s 271B. Exemption may be denied for year without audit report.
redFlags.correction: Engage a CA to audit trust accounts and file Form 10B/10BB electronically before due date.

redFlags.title: Corpus Donations Included in Taxable Income
redFlags.description: Voluntary contributions made specifically towards corpus fund are capital receipts — NOT income. Including corpus donations in income overstates income and distorts application ratio.
redFlags.consequence: Incorrect application ratio; potential refund claim needed.
redFlags.correction: Maintain a separate corpus fund account. Track corpus receipts separately. Invest corpus in specified modes u/s 11(5).

redFlags.title: Income Applied Outside Charitable Objects
redFlags.description: Trust can only apply income for purposes specified in the trust deed (charitable objects). Applying funds for activities outside these objects — even if charitable in nature — violates the trust deed.
redFlags.consequence: Such expenditure does not count as 'application' — reduces application ratio and may trigger taxation.
redFlags.correction: Consult trust deed for objects. Amend objects with proper legal process if scope needs expansion.

redFlags.title: Anonymous Donations Exceeding 5% of Total Donations (Section 115BBC)
redFlags.description: Anonymous donations (where donor identity not maintained) exceeding 5% of total donations OR ₹1 lakh (whichever is higher) are taxed at 30% flat u/s 115BBC for charitable trusts (excluding religious trusts).
redFlags.consequence: 30% tax on excess anonymous donations with no deductions or exemptions.
redFlags.correction: Maintain donor records for all significant donations. Discourage anonymous donations. Religious trusts are exempt from 115BBC.

redFlags.title: Excessive Payments to Trustees or Related Parties
redFlags.description: Sections 13(1)(c) and 13(1)(d): If trust income/property benefits a trustee, settlor, or specified persons (relatives, companies in which they have interest) more than reasonably, exemption can be denied.
redFlags.consequence: Entire exemption u/s 11 denied for the year. Full income taxed.
redFlags.correction: Trustees' remuneration should be reasonable and in public interest. Avoid property use by trustees at below-market rent.

redFlags.title: Not Filing Form 10/9A When Accumulating Beyond 15%
redFlags.description: If trust cannot apply full 85% in the current year and wants to accumulate the excess for future use, Form 10 (for specific project accumulation, up to 5 years) or Form 9A (for one-year deferral) must be filed before the ITR due date.
redFlags.consequence: Accumulated amount taxable at 30% if forms not filed in time.
redFlags.correction: File Form 10 or Form 9A electronically before ITR due date (usually July 31 for non-audit trusts; Oct 31 for audit trusts).

validationErrors.cause: Trust did not enter valid registration number in Part A
validationErrors.resolution: Enter 12AB registration number from Income Tax portal. Verify registration is currently valid.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Form 10B audit report not filed for income > ₹1 Crore
validationErrors.cause: Trust income exceeded ₹1Cr but audit report not uploaded
validationErrors.resolution: File Form 10B/10BB through CA login on Income Tax portal before ITR submission

validationErrors.errorMessage: Application of income less than 85% — tax liability triggered
validationErrors.cause: Schedule ER shows < 85% of income applied with no Form 10/9A accumulation
validationErrors.resolution: File Form 10 for project accumulation OR pay 30% tax on shortfall in Part B-TTI

validationErrors.errorMessage: Political party return missing Election Commission registration details
validationErrors.cause: Mandatory fields for 139(4B) filers not filled
validationErrors.resolution: Enter ECI registration number and date of registration in Part A fields
